_comment: HELD KEY for the novelty-reserve pressure. The measure (scripts/novelty-reserve.mjs) never reads this; scripts/novelty-reserve-score.mjs applies it. Read the CONTROL first, then the per-stream split, then stability.

roles.stream_a: stable — a sealed 3-name cast recurring; the last newcomer before the cursor was 17 lines ago. Recent novelty rate ~0.
roles.stream_b: stale_burst — 11 newcomers front-loaded (lines 0-10), then a quiet recurrence tail. The novelty is real but STALE at the cursor.
roles.stream_c: recent_churn — the SAME counts as stream_b (same distinct-name count, intro count, recurrence count, total mass), but the 10 roster newcomers arrive LATE (lines 9-18), right up to the cursor. Recent novelty rate high.

control.claim: stream_b and stream_c are identical under every COUNT-based surface feature — same number of distinct names (12), same intros (12), same recurrences (8), same total γ-mass at the cursor — and differ ONLY in the temporal order of the same events. So (a) the CURRENT reserve 1/(total+1) reads them identically, and (b) any cheat keying on a cumulative count reads them identically. Only a recency-decay-weighted reserve can separate them. If the signal-derived reserve separates b from c, the separation cannot be a count artifact.
control.current_path_is_flat: Under the default (pre-fix) path the observable channels at the cursor — bayesBits, surprisalBits, and the constant reserve 1/(total+1) — are ~equal across ALL THREE streams (the gap: the live engine is blind to the recent novelty rate). 'flat' tolerance: spread < 0.02.

dissociation.channel: reserve (the signal-derived reserve share nu/(total+nu), emitted under readingAt(.,{adaptiveReserve:true}))
dissociation.claim: reserve(stream_c) > reserve(stream_b) > reserve(stream_a): the reserved amplitude tracks the recent novelty rate. The count-matched gap is large — reserve(stream_c) >= 3 * reserve(stream_b) — proving recency, not count, drives it.
dissociation.amplitude: noveltyMass(stream_c) > noveltyMass(stream_b) > noveltyMass(stream_a) — the γ-decayed mass of recent newcomer admissions, the amplitude that goes into the fixed Born step.

consequence.channel: bayesBits at the cursor under {adaptiveReserve:true} (the newcomer's significance)
consequence.claim: A lower reserve makes the newcomer a larger violation: the sealed-cast newcomer moves belief MORE than the fresh-churn newcomer — bayesBits(stream_a) > bayesBits(stream_c). (b-vs-c on this channel is shape-confounded by the differing incumbent profiles and is NOT asserted; the confound-free statement lives on the reserve channel above and on the core isolation lock in tests/novelty-reserve.test.js.)

stability.parity: The DEFAULT path is byte-identical to pre-fix: readingAt(doc, at) with no adaptiveReserve flag returns no reserve/noveltyMass/totalMass fields and the same bayesBits/surprisalBits it always did. The opt-in is the only thing that threads the signal-derived amplitude through the (unchanged) Born step.
stability.surface_invariance: The reserve reads novelty STRUCTURE, not the surface verbs: the recurrence lines cycle through different intransitive verbs and the reserve is unaffected — it is a function of newcomer admission timing alone.

mechanism_tag: signal-derived-reserve-amplitude: the reserve is the γ-decayed mass of recent NEWCOMER admissions (same γ as the figure field), run through the SAME fixed Born step (reserve = nu/(Σ+nu)). Context enters at the amplitude; the law stays put. Replaces the hand-rolled NOVELTY_RESERVE = 1.0 constant. Layer: interior (src/core/surprise.js noveltyAmplitude), threaded opt-in via src/perceiver/reading.js.
